content:
# Joshua Chapter 19 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 242-244 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_019_page_001.jpg`

1. And the second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of the Sons of Simeon according to their clans. And their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the Sons of Judah.

2. And they had as their inheritance: Beer-sheba, and Sheba, and Moladah,

3. and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem,

4. and Eltolad, and Bethul, and Hormah,

5. and Ziklag, and Beth-marcaboth, and Hazar-susah,

6. and Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen — thirteen cities and their villages.

7. Ain, Rimmon, and Ether, and Ashan — four cities and their villages.

8. And all the villages that were around these cities as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negev. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Simeon according to their clans.

9. From the allotment of the Sons of Judah was the inheritance of the Sons of Simeon, for the portion of the Sons of Judah was too great for them. So the Sons of Simeon received their inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.

10. And the third lot came up for the Sons of Zebulun according to their clans. And the border of their inheritance extended as far as Sarid.

11. And their border went up westward, even to Maralah, and reached to Dabbesheth, and reached to the wadi that is before Jokneam.

12. And it turned from Sarid eastward toward the rising of the sun, to the border of Chisloth-tabor, and went out to Daberath, and went up to Japhia.

13. And from there it passed along eastward to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and went out to Rimmon, which bends toward Neah.

14. And the border turned around it on the north to Hannathon, and its outgoings were at the Valley of Iphtah-el.

15. And Kattath, and Nahalal, and Shimron, and Idalah, and Bethlehem — twelve cities and their villages.

16. This was the inheritance of the Sons of Zebulun according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

17. For Issachar the fourth lot came out, for the Sons of Issachar according to their clans.

18. And their territory included Jezreel, and Chesulloth, and Shunem,

19. and Hapharaim, and Shion, and Anaharath,

20. and Rabbith, and Kishion, and Ebez,

21. and Remeth, and En-gannim, and En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.

22. And the border reached to Tabor, and Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and the outgoings of their border were at the Jordan — sixteen cities and their villages.

23. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Issachar according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

24. And the fifth lot came out for the tribe of the Sons of Asher according to their clans.

25. And their territory included Helkath, and Hali, and Beten, and Achshaph,

26. and Allammelech, and Amad, and Mishal. And it reached to Carmel on the west, and to Shihor-libnath.

27. And it turned toward the rising of the sun to Beth-dagon, and reached to Zebulun and to the Valley of Iphtah-el northward, to Beth-emek and Neiel, and went out to Cabul on the left.

28. And Ebron, and Rehob, and Hammon, and Kanah, as far as Great Sidon.

29. And the border turned to Ramah, and to the fortified city of Tyre. And the border turned to Hosah, and its outgoings were at the sea, from the region of Achzib.

30. And Ummah, and Aphek, and Rehob — twenty-two cities and their villages.

31. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Asher according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

32. For the Sons of Naphtali the sixth lot came out, for the Sons of Naphtali according to their clans.

33. And their border was from Heleph, from the oak in Zaanannim, and Adami-nekeb, and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum, and its outgoings were at the Jordan.

34. And the border turned westward to Aznoth-tabor, and went out from there to Hukkok, and reached to Zebulun on the south, and reached to Asher on the west, and to Judah at the Jordan toward the rising of the sun.

35. And the fortified cities were: Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth,

36. and Adamah, and Ramah, and Hazor,

37. and Kedesh, and Edrei, and En-hazor,

38. and Iron, and Migdal-el, Horem, and Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh — nineteen cities and their villages.

39. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Naphtali according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

40. For the tribe of the Sons of Dan according to their clans, the seventh lot came out.

41. And the territory of their inheritance was Zorah, and Eshtaol, and Ir-shemesh,

42. and Shaalabbin, and Aijalon, and Ithlah,

43. and Elon, and Timnah, and Ekron,

44. and Eltekeh, and Gibbethon, and Baalath,

45. and Jehud, and Bene-berak, and Gath-rimmon,

46. and Me-jarkon, and Rakkon, with the border opposite Joppa.

47. And the territory of the Sons of Dan went out beyond them, for the Sons of Dan went up and fought against Leshem and captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword and possessed it and dwelt in it. And they called Leshem "Dan," after the name of Dan their father.

48. This was the inheritance of the tribe of the Sons of Dan according to their clans — these cities and their villages.

49. And they finished distributing the land as an inheritance according to its borders. And the Sons of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun in their midst.

50. By the command of YHWH they gave him the city that he asked for — Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim. And he built the city and dwelt in it.

51. These are the inheritances that Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the Sons of Israel distributed by lot in Shiloh before YHWH, at the entrance of the tent of meeting. So they finished dividing the land.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (יהוה):** Rendered as "YHWH" in vv. 50-51, preserving the Tetragrammaton. The divine name appears only at the chapter's conclusion — in the authorization of Joshua's personal inheritance and in the summary of the entire land-distribution process at Shiloh.

2. **"Sons of" (בְּנֵי):** Consistently rendered "Sons of Simeon," "Sons of Zebulun," "Sons of Issachar," "Sons of Asher," "Sons of Naphtali," "Sons of Dan," and "Sons of Israel" per LOGOS convention, rather than the interpretive "Simeonites," "Zebulunites," etc.

3. **Simeon within Judah (vv. 1, 9):** The Hebrew text explicitly states Simeon's inheritance was בְּתוֹךְ (*betokh*, "in the midst of") the inheritance of Judah. Verse 9 provides the rationale: Judah's portion was רַב (*rav*, "too great/too much") for them. This fulfills Jacob's prophecy of Simeon's scattering (Gen. 49:7).

4. **"Bethlehem" in v. 15:** This is Zebulunite Bethlehem (בֵּית לֶחֶם), a town in the Galilee distinct from the more famous Bethlehem of Judah. The Hebrew is identical in both cases.

5. **Place name variants:** Several cities in this chapter appear under different forms elsewhere in the Hebrew Bible. For example: "Sheba" (v. 2) may be a textual variant of "Beer-sheba" (cf. Josh. 15:26); "Bethul" (v. 4) appears as "Bethuel" in 1 Chr. 4:30; "Sharuhen" (v. 6) appears as "Shaaraim" in 1 Chr. 4:31. LOGOS follows the consonantal text of the Leningrad Codex in each instance.

6. **"went out beyond them" (v. 47):** The Hebrew וַיֵּצֵא גְבוּל־בְּנֵי־דָן מֵהֶם is rendered "went out beyond them," indicating that the Danite territory proved insufficient. The subsequent narrative of Dan's migration northward to Leshem (called "Laish" in Judges 18) is one of the most significant geographical notices in Joshua, explaining the relocation of an entire tribe.

7. **"Timnath-serah" (v. 50):** The Leningrad Codex reads תִּמְנַת־סֶרַח (*Timnath-serah*). In Judges 2:9 the same city appears as תִּמְנַת־חֶרֶס (*Timnath-heres*), with the consonants of the second element reversed. Both forms are attested in the Masoretic tradition.

8. **City counts:** The chapter records: Simeon — 13 + 4 = 17 cities (vv. 6-7); Zebulun — 12 cities (v. 15); Issachar — 16 cities (v. 22); Asher — 22 cities (v. 30); Naphtali — 19 cities (v. 38). Dan's cities are listed without a total. These enumerations do not always match the names counted in the lists, a well-known feature of the boundary descriptions throughout Joshua 13-21.

9. **Structural note:** Joshua 19 concludes the tribal allotment section that began in chapter 13. Verse 51 forms an inclusio with 14:1, both mentioning Eleazar, Joshua, the tribal heads, lot-casting, and Shiloh. Joshua's own inheritance (vv. 49-50) is pointedly placed last — the leader receives his portion only after every tribe has been settled.
